description: NHP agent-registration Noise-handshake golden packets (X25519 / AES-256-GCM / BLAKE2s): the OTP request, the emailed-code and pre-issued-key REG requests, and the server register-ack (RAK) success and error replies.
notes: otp / reg_emailed / reg_preissued: DETERMINISTIC. A conformant initiator MUST reproduce packet_hex byte-for-byte from the listed inputs (fixed ephemeral_priv, counter, timestamp_nanos, preamble). These packets are decrypt-fenced by the NHP Go server responder, which independently recovers the initiator static key, timestamp, and body.

OTP body contract: the NHP_OTP body is {usrId, devId, aspId, pass} in that field order (pass carries the API-key secret). The order matches the live agent implementations byte-for-byte (Go otpRequestBody, the TS OTP request body), so a conformant initiator that marshals the same struct reproduces body_hex exactly.

REG body contract: the NHP_REG body is {usrId, devId, aspId, otp, usrData}, where usrData is the registration audit metadata {hostname, version, takeover}. Fields are omitted when empty/false (omitempty), so takeover appears only when true. This matches the live agent implementations byte-for-byte (Go registerRequestBody/registerUserData, the TS register body, the OpenNHP server register-metadata decoder).

reg_emailed vs reg_preissued: both are NHP_REG with the same framing; they differ in the body `otp` value (an emailed 8-digit code vs a pre-issued key secret) and in usrData.takeover (reg_emailed omits it — takeover=false; reg_preissued sets takeover=true). A verifier distinguishes the two enrollment paths by the matched key record, not by the packet bytes. Documented so a consumer sees both paths use the same NHP_REG packet framing.

rak_success / rak_error: FROZEN decrypt-only. The server sealed these with a RANDOM server ephemeral, so they are NOT reproducible by a client; a consumer can only DECRYPT them and MUST recover type=NHP_RAK (14), the counter, the timestamp_nanos, and the body. Re-hosted here verbatim as frozen golden values, mirroring the relay-knock `ack` precedent.

RAK body contract: the NHP_RAK body is {errCode, errMsg, aspId} in that field order (ServerRegisterAckMsg); errMsg is omitted when empty (omitempty), so rak_success is {errCode, aspId} and rak_error carries all three. errCode "0" (or empty) is success; a "521xx" code is a typed denial. The order matches the OpenNHP reference server byte-for-byte.

conformance#19 matched-counter pair: rak_success AND rak_error carry counter_hex = 0xb, which ECHOES reg_emailed.counter (11). The reg_emailed -> rak pair is therefore counter-matched, so a consumer validating the counter-echo enforcement (a RAK must echo its REG's transaction counter) has a positive fixture.

Origin: OTP/REG built by a type-parameterized NHP initiator with a fixed ephemeral/counter/timestamp (a conformant initiator reproduces packet_hex byte-for-byte); RAK sealed by the OpenNHP reference server with a fixed transaction counter and a random ephemeral, so it is frozen decrypt-only. Every body (otp/reg/rak) is serialized in its real-struct field order (otpRequestBody / registerRequestBody / ServerRegisterAckMsg), so the vectors are byte-faithful to the live implementations. Keys/ids/secrets are SYNTHETIC (fixed fill patterns, distinct from the relay-knock set) — no real keys, emails, or hostnames.

Fence status: the authoritative independent fence for these packets today is the OpenNHP reference server decrypt suite, which recovers the initiator static key / timestamp / type / body from every case. An in-repo BEHAVIORAL fence that rebuilds the deterministic OTP/REG packet_hex and re-decrypts it (as relay_knock's does) is deferred until a type-parameterized single-message builder is exposed to this repo's verify SDK; tracked in layervai/qurl-conformance#21.
